target: data/sections.json -> part8b.html -> details block id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers" (Section 8b.3 — eclipse layered audit). Replace the entire details block to incorporate the curmudgeon major hole #12 three-part edit: (1) explicit non-discrimination sentence in the L3a body, (2) extension of the (d) Unchanged clause from one item to two, (3) adoption-label change from 'ADOPTED (FULL)' to 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)'. Also promotes the non-discrimination critique into the ps-tldr summary line so a skim-reader sees it before expanding, and addresses the Fail-clause tension (SQ variation cannot be 'separable alternative' when Chapman IS the SQ-dynamo).
patch_instructions: In data/sections.json, locate the part8b.html string. Find the details block whose id is p8b-3-eclipse-layers. Replace the entire block (from the opening <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> through its matching </details>) with the replacement_html provided. The block boundaries are: starts at <details id="p8b-3-eclipse-layers"> and ends at the </details> that immediately precedes <details id="p8b-4-raw-data-limits">. Preserve the leading newline (or other whitespace) before and the newline after the block — only the block itself is replaced. No other section is modified by this expansion.
replacement_html:
8b.3 Point 3 — Audit the eclipse stack in layers: ensemble anchor, Mohe timing, Tier 3 falsifier, and the open optics caveat.

Adopted as steel-man: we restructure our eclipse content into the four-layer presentation and publish the Tier 3 magnetic threshold explicitly. Two structural critiques survive adoption — an ensemble “anchor” fit to the data it anchors is calibration, not anchor; and the Tier 3 magnetic Pass band itself does not distinguish dome from globe (Chapman’s Sq-dynamo mechanism predicts the same −17 to −21 nT range, calibrated on the same Kim & Chang 2018 ensemble).

(a) Separate the eclipse claim into four layers: (L1) ensemble anchor — WIN-068 (39-eclipse ensemble); (L2) Mohe timing — WIN-011 (1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse); (L3a) Tier 3 magnetic falsifier — PRED-095 (August 2026 eclipse); (L3b) Tier 3 gravity — PRED-TIER-2 (recycled WIN-011); (L4) open optics caveat — the logged ray-trace gap. (b) Layered audits force explicit statements about what would falsify the claim. A falsifier that never triggers is not a falsifier; spelling out the Tier 3 threshold is due diligence.

(c) Under adoption we publish a four-row stack. L1 (WIN-068): current verdict Std Model Explains — “the Chapman mechanism (90+ years old) predicts the entire ensemble signal without dome parameters; the dome adds no derivation — only a post-hoc scaling formula calibrated against observed eclipse data.” Under adoption this is explicitly labeled a calibration layer, not an anchor. L2 (WIN-011): current verdict Not Demonstrated — the 6–7 µGal Wang signal is within 1997-era LaCoste-Romberg instrumental noise; Van Camp et al. (2001) found no eclipse gravity signal with four superconducting gravimeters (~1,000× more precise). L3a (PRED-095, magnetic): Genuinely prospective (registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending). Under adoption we publish an explicit magnetic threshold — “Pass = observed −17 to −21 nT at >80% coverage stations, ≥3 independent sites, pre-registered instruments; Fail = outside this range, or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone.” A Pass in this band does not distinguish dome from globe — both predict signals in this range; only the dome claims a novel derivation path, and WIN-068's code_analysis tag (derives_from_dome:false) confirms there is no independent dome derivation to distinguish. The dome's Pass band is moreover calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble that L1 already characterizes as Std Model Explains; the numerical specificity of the threshold is a product of shared training data, not an independent dome forecast. The Fail-clause concession that “signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone” would falsify the claim is in tension with the Pass criterion: Chapman's mechanism is the SQ-dynamo, so a confirmed signal in this band is consistent with both models simultaneously, and the Fail-clause cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause's framing. If the dome has not pinned the threshold to this precision in its own published materials, we cite the gap as our finding. L3b (PRED-TIER-2, gravity): Recycled — restates WIN-011 with 1997 Wang et al. gravity-eclipse data; the falsification criterion for this layer is independent replication of the gravity signal at the 2026 eclipse sites. L4 (optics): the dome already logs the ray-trace gap; we credit the disclosure. A logged gap is not a solved gap.

(d) Adopted: layered presentation, explicit falsifier threshold. Unchanged: (i) an ensemble anchor fit to the data it “anchors” is the OTS-wrong-side pattern in physics form — the right operation applied to the wrong side; an anchor is what data hits from outside, a fit is what data draws toward itself (see the Timestamp Error tab); (ii) L3a's Pass band is the Chapman (1933) Sq-dynamo prediction calibrated on the same Kim & Chang (2018) ensemble that anchors L1 — a Pass does not distinguish dome from globe, and there is no independent dome derivation (WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false) that would let the Pass criterion arbitrate between the two models.

ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man): four-layer eclipse stack as audit format; ensemble-anchor and L3a non-discrimination critiques unchanged.
holes_addressed: SEC-8b.c1 hole #12 (major) — 8b.3 L3a publishes PRED-095 magnetic Pass threshold without applying our own non-discrimination thesis; Chapman's Sq-dynamo predicts the same -17 to -21 nT band; Pass does not distinguish dome from globe; WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false confirms no independent dome derivation. Three-part recommended edit fully implemented (body sentence + (d) Unchanged extension + adoption-label change). TLDR also updated to surface the critique before expansion (curmudgeon's optional follow-on). Fail-clause logical tension surfaced explicitly as the curmudgeon optionally suggested.
new_evidence_added: Cross-link from L3a body to WIN-068's code_analysis tag (derives_from_dome:false) — making the architectural foreclosure concrete in-line, not just referenced.

Explicit acknowledgement that the Pass band's numerical specificity (-17 to -21 nT) derives from calibration on the same Kim & Chang (2018) 39-eclipse ensemble that anchors L1. Surfaces the shared-training-data origin of the threshold.

Explicit treatment of the Fail-clause tension: 'or signal explained by ionospheric SQ variation alone' cannot be triggered without abandoning the Pass-clause framing, because Chapman's mechanism IS the SQ-dynamo. The escape clause folds in on itself.

anticipated_objections.objection: The Pass band -17 to -21 nT is sharper than 'Chapman generically predicts'; a quantitative threshold differs from a qualitative mechanism.
anticipated_objections.response: The numerical specificity is the product of calibration against Kim & Chang (2018) — the same 39-eclipse ensemble that L1 (WIN-068) characterizes as Std Model Explains. Sharing the training data with the standard model means a tight Pass band is a fit, not an independent derivation. The dome would need to derive -17 to -21 nT from its own cavity geometry without using the Kim & Chang ensemble as input; WIN-068's code_analysis (derives_from_dome:false) records that this has not been done.

anticipated_objections.objection: We are foreclosing an honest prospective falsifier. PRED-095 is genuinely registered 2026-04-04, genuinely prospective, and the dome is willing to be wrong if measurements come in outside -17 to -21 nT.
anticipated_objections.response: We do not retract the prospective status of PRED-095 — the four-layer presentation publishes its registration window and pending verdict. What changes is the interpretation of a Pass: a Pass that both models predict (and that the dome has calibrated to within the same data the standard model already explains) confirms neither model preferentially. The (d) Unchanged clause makes this explicit so readers do not over-credit a possible August 2026 Pass.

anticipated_objections.objection: Calling the SQ-variation Fail-clause 'logically unable to trigger' is too strong. A signal can be in the -17 to -21 nT band AND have features inconsistent with pure SQ-dynamo (e.g., timing offsets, latitudinal pattern mismatches).
anticipated_objections.response: Granted in principle, and we soften from 'logically impossible' to 'in tension with the Pass criterion' in the body text. The deeper point survives: the dome's published Fail-clause text treats SQ variation as a separable alternative, but the very mechanism that produces signals in this band IS the SQ-dynamo. If a 2026 measurement shows a -17 to -21 nT signal with morphology inconsistent with Chapman, the Fail-clause-against-SQ would activate — but distinguishing that fine-grained morphology requires far more than what PRED-095's published criteria specify.

anticipated_objections.objection: By promoting the non-discrimination critique to the ps-tldr, we are making the section read as a rejection rather than as Adopted. The point of 8b is to charitably engage the dome author's audit list.
anticipated_objections.response: The label change to 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)' parallels 8b.2's existing label and signals the same posture: the audit format is adopted, the substantive critique is unchanged. The TLDR honestly reports both outcomes. Hiding the critique in the body while the TLDR proclaims plain Adoption would be the rhetorical move; surfacing it is the honest one.

kernel_of_truth: The dome author's request that we audit the eclipse claim in layers (ensemble anchor / Mohe timing / Tier 3 falsifier / open optics) is genuinely good audit hygiene. Forcing explicit statements about what would falsify each layer is the correct epistemic move; spelling out a numerical Pass band for PRED-095 is a real commitment that lets observers grade the prediction. The four-layer audit format is a methodological improvement we adopt without modification. What does not survive is the implicit framing that publishing an explicit Pass band makes PRED-095 a discriminating test — the threshold sits where Chapman's Sq-dynamo mechanism already predicts signals, calibrated on the same ensemble the standard model already explains, and there is no independent dome derivation (WIN-068 derives_from_dome:false) that would let the band arbitrate between models. Audit format adopted; non-discrimination critique survives.
cross_references: WIN-068 (data/wins.json) — Std Model Explains; finding text 'Non-discriminating: Chapman (1933) ionospheric mechanism predicts eclipse magnetic perturbations across all 39 eclipses in the ensemble'; code_analysis.derives_from_dome:false
PRED-095 (data/predictions.json) — registered 2026-04-04, window closes 2026-08-12, verdict pending
Part 4.2 Eclipse subsection — title 'A Sophisticated But Non-Discriminating Prediction' carries the same architectural critique that this patch forwards into 8b.3 L3a
Section 8b.2 — provides the 'ADOPTED (FULL as steel-man)' label pattern this patch mirrors

unaddressed_holes_note: This expansion addresses ONLY hole #12 from SEC-8b.c1 (the major non-discrimination hole that ISS-1190 was filed against). The other 11 holes from that review (4.2 collision, missing CSS, citation-compliance audit, claim_key/resolution-criteria unshipped, 8b.3 L2 µGal mislabel which was already fixed by EXP-185, 8b.2 Row 2 overclaim, 8b.3 L1 calibration framing, hero phrase, 0.4% Nullarbor source, chg-006 omission, PRED-095/PRED-TIER-1 disambiguation, conditional escape clauses) remain open and should be tracked under separate issue/expansion entries by the decider.
scoreboard_note: L3a's Pass band is a clean illustration of the take-him-at-his-word scoring problem the foundational HNOTE-SCOREBOARD-FOUNDATIONAL-001 wants to surface — when the dome's published prediction is identical (within uncertainty) to the globe's published prediction, win_by_own_standard may be 'yes' but discriminating is 'no'. PRED-095 belongs in the Phase 1 15-row scoreboard with provenance:'forward_with_globe_inputs' (Chapman ensemble calibration) and a row_note flagging the shared-training-data origin. Cross-reference flagged for the Phase 1 draft (EXP-190) so the row treatment of PRED-095 lands consistent with this 8b.3 patch.

Decider should integrate by replacing the p8b-3-eclipse-layers details block in data/sections.json with the replacement_html provided here, then close ISS-1190.
